word_list.word: [help] a lame dog over a stile
word_list.pronunciation: hˈɛlp ɐ lˈeɪm dˈɑːɡ ˌoʊvɚɹ ɐ stˈaɪl

senses.definition: to offer help to someone who cannot provide their basic needs for themselves or are in an extremely difficult situation

examples.x: Despite the simple request, he always went out of his way to help a lame dog over a stile, demonstrating his compassion for even the smallest needs.
She never hesitated to lend a hand, whether it was a major problem or a minor inconvenience; she would always help a lame dog over a stile.
The organization's mission was to help a lame dog over a stile, focusing on providing support and resources to those facing everyday challenges.
He believed in the power of small acts of kindness and would go out of his way to help a lame dog over a stile, knowing that even the tiniest gesture could make a difference.
The community rallied together to help a lame dog over a stile by organizing fundraisers, offering assistance, and providing a network of support for those in need.

word_list.pronunciation: lˈɛnd ɔːɹ ɡˈɪv ˌɛsbˈiː ɐ hˈɛlpɪŋ ɔːɹ hˈænd

senses.definition: to give a person help or assistance in doing something
to protect or hide another person's mistakes or misdeeds, often by providing a false or misleading account of the situation
a way of showing one's support or approval of someone by shouting loudly to encourage them or inviting others to do the same

examples.x: After the team's victory, the crowd erupted in three cheers for their favorite player.
As the newlyweds walked down the aisle, the guests filled the air with three cheers for the happy couple.
The audience gave three cheers for the performer's outstanding musical performance.
The school principal received three cheers from the students during the assembly for her dedicated leadership.
The fans gathered at the stadium raised their voices in three cheers for the star athlete's record-breaking achievement.

senses.definition: to be strict, especially toward someone close to one, to help them improve or become stronger

examples.x: As a coach, I had to play the bad guy and push my team during training to ensure they reached their full potential on the field.
My older brother played the bad guy by challenging me to take on more responsibilities and not letting me settle for mediocrity.
The tough professor played the bad guy by giving strict assignments and holding high expectations to push the students towards academic excellence.
The mentor played the bad guy by providing constructive criticism and pushing their mentee outside of their comfort zone to foster professional growth.
The manager played the bad guy by refusing to accept subpar performance and consistently pushing their employees to deliver their best work.

senses.definition: to stop acting passively and start to have a helpful role in doing something

examples.x: Instead of complaining, why don't you make yourself useful and help clean up the mess?
The teenager decided to make herself useful by offering to walk the neighbor's dog while they were away.
During the team project, everyone was encouraged to make themselves useful by contributing their ideas and skills.
Instead of watching TV all day, why not make yourself useful and mow the lawn?
The volunteer eagerly made himself useful by assisting with organizing the charity event.

senses.definition: to say or do something with the intention of helping someone even though it might hurt their feelings or seem unkind

examples.x: The parents decided to be cruel to be kind and enforce strict rules on their teenager to ensure they learned discipline and responsibility.
The coach was tough on the team, pushing them to their limits in training, but it was all in an effort to be cruel to be kind and help them achieve their full potential.
Sometimes teachers have to be cruel to be kind by giving challenging assignments and holding high expectations to foster academic growth in their students.
The therapist employed a tough love approach, being cruel to be kind, in order to help the patient confront deep-seated issues and promote healing.

The intervention was emotionally difficult for everyone involved, but they knew they had to be cruel to be kind and confront the person about their destructive behavior for the sake of their well-being.

word_list.pronunciation: ɹˈaɪd ɪn ˌɑːn ɐ wˈaɪt hˈɔːɹs

senses.definition: to suddenly appear to help someone

examples.x: Just when we thought we were doomed, a generous philanthropist rode in on a white horse and saved our struggling community center with a substantial donation.
The CEO rode in on a white horse, making bold decisions and implementing innovative strategies that turned the company around from the brink of bankruptcy.
When the team was losing hope, the star player returned from injury and rode in on a white horse, leading them to a remarkable comeback victory.
When the project was falling behind schedule, the experienced consultant rode in on a white horse, offering expert guidance and turning the situation around with remarkable efficiency.

senses.definition: in the process of being taken care of or dealt with by someone who has the skill or experience

examples.x: After researching various options, we decided to leave our beloved pet in good hands by choosing a reputable and caring pet boarding facility.
The company entrusted the important project to their experienced team of professionals, knowing that it was in safe hands for successful execution.
As I boarded the plane, the pilot's reassuring voice over the intercom assured us that we were in good hands for a safe and smooth flight.
The parents felt at ease leaving their child at the daycare center, knowing that the dedicated and nurturing staff would ensure their little one was in safe hands.
The elderly woman found comfort in knowing that her financial affairs were in good hands with the trustworthy and knowledgeable financial advisor.

senses.definition: to be willing to provide someone with help and support whenever they need it

examples.x: When my friend went through a difficult breakup, I promised to be there for her, lending a listening ear and offering support.
My best friend always knows she can count on me to be there for her, whether it's celebrating her successes or comforting her during tough times.

word_list.pronunciation: hæv wˈʌnz bˈɛst ˈɪntɹəsts æt hˈɑːɹt

senses.definition: to show concern about someone, often someone who is in trouble, and do one's best to help them

examples.x: My parents always had my best interests at heart, supporting my dreams and aspirations every step of the way.
When negotiating a business deal, it's important to work with someone who truly has your interests at heart and will strive for a mutually beneficial outcome.
I chose a therapist who genuinely had my best interests at heart, providing personalized guidance and helping me navigate through challenging times.
Despite the differences, my best friend has always had my interests at heart, offering honest advice and looking out for my well-being.

senses.definition: an act that can be helpful to someone

examples.x: When I was feeling overwhelmed with work, my colleague did me a good turn by offering to help complete some of my tasks.
The stranger's act of returning my lost wallet without expecting any reward was a remarkable example of a genuine good turn.
My friend did me a good turn by lending me their car when mine broke down, ensuring I could attend an important event.
The neighbor always does good turns for everyone on our street, whether it's shoveling snow from driveways or running errands for the elderly.
When I was sick, my roommate did me a good turn by preparing meals and taking care of household chores, allowing me to rest and recover.

senses.definition: an encouraging and motivational speech or conversation given to boost someone's enthusiasm, confidence, or determination, especially before a challenging task or event

examples.x: Before the championship match, the coach gathered the team for a pep talk, inspiring them to give their all and believe in their abilities.
As I prepared for a challenging presentation, my colleague gave me a pep talk, reminding me of my strengths and boosting my confidence.
The teacher delivered a heartfelt pep talk to the students before their final exams, encouraging them to stay focused and believe in their capabilities.
After a series of setbacks, my friend gave me a much-needed pep talk, reminding me to persevere and not let failure define my journey.
Before the job interview, my mentor provided a pep talk, reminding me to showcase my skills, exude confidence, and seize the opportunity.

senses.definition: in a weakened state and in desperate need of help

examples.x: She was on her knees, pleading for forgiveness after her mistake.
The relentless drought had farmers on their knees, praying for rain.

senses.definition: a person or a place that can help one overcome a difficult situation

examples.x: In the midst of her emotional turmoil, her best friend became a port in a storm, offering a listening ear and unwavering support.
The experienced lawyer proved to be a port in a storm for the defendant, skillfully navigating the complex legal proceedings.

senses.definition: to surrender oneself to the mercy or control of another

examples.x: I lie before you today, humbled and desperate, begging for your assistance.

senses.definition: to save someone or something from a difficult or dangerous situation

examples.x: His quick thinking and negotiation skills allowed him to pull the fat from the fire and salvage the failing business deal.
In the midst of the storm, the coast guard's rescue team pulled the fat from the fire by saving the stranded sailors.
The firefighter's heroic actions pulled the fat from the fire, rescuing the family from the burning building just in time.
With their star player injured, the underdog team needed a miracle to win, and their rookie player pulled the fat from the fire with a last-minute, game-winning goal.
The tech support team worked tirelessly to pull the fat from the fire and recover the company's critical data after a cyberattack.
